articles.title: Monte Carlo Rally 2026: Night Stages, New Cars, Same Drama
articles.summary: A detailed recap of the 2026 Monte Carlo Rally with onboard insights, tyre gambles, and how the latest Rally1 evolutions handled the icy tarmac.
articles.content: The 2026 Monte Carlo Rally delivered everything fans hoped for: unpredictable weather, bold tyre calls, and the first full-season evolution kits for the Rally1 hybrids. From the opening night stages above Monaco to the decisive Sunday Power Stage, the event was shaped by changing grip and changing fortunes...

articles.title: Monte Carlo Rally 2025: Season-Opening Storylines to Watch
articles.summary: Previewing the key stories, tyre strategies, and dark horses ahead of the 2025 Monte Carlo Rally season opener.
articles.content: The World Rally Championship returns to the mountains above Monaco for the 2025 season opener. The Monte Carlo Rally remains the toughest way to start a campaign, with crews balancing studded tyres, slicks, and everything in between...

articles.title: From Col de Turini to Hybrid Power: The Evolving Monte Carlo Rally
articles.summary: A long-form feature on how the Monte Carlo Rally evolved from regularity run to cutting-edge hybrid WRC showpiece.
articles.content: Few events in motorsport have a history as rich as the Monte Carlo Rally. Born as a reliability and navigation challenge, Monte has transformed into a modern tarmac rally with icy surprises still lurking in the shadows of the Alps...

articles.title: Rally Finland 2023: Why the Jumps Still Matter
articles.summary: An in-depth look at Rally Finland 2023, the physics of the famous jumps, and how modern aero has changed the game.
articles.content: Rally Finland has always been about commitment. In 2023, that commitment is filtered through complex aerodynamics, hybrid deployment strategies, and precise pacenotes. Yet, at its heart, the event still rewards bravery over the crests...

articles.title: Gravel Masterclass: How WRC Drivers Read the Road in 2023
articles.summary: A driver-focused feature on car placement, ruts, and loose surface tactics across the 2023 gravel rallies.
articles.content: Watching onboard footage from the 2023 gravel rallies, it’s easy to miss the subtle corrections that separate a clean run from a stage-winning time. Drivers are constantly reading surface changes, negotiating ruts, and adjusting their lines...

articles.title: Inside the 2023 WRC Tyre War: Compounds, Cuts, and Calculated Risks
articles.summary: Technical deep dive into tyre choices and how they decided key rallies during the 2023 WRC season.
articles.content: Tyres remain the most critical variable in rallying. In 2023, with extreme weather swings and ever-tighter tyre allocations, the risk-reward balance moved closer to the edge...

articles.title: From Juniors to Rally1: 2023’s Most Unique Career Paths
articles.summary: Exploring how three drivers carved unusual routes to the top level of rallying by 2023.
articles.content: Not every Rally1 driver followed the traditional path of national championships and Junior WRC titles. In 2023, several names on the entry list came from rallycross, circuit racing, or even esports...

articles.title: Memories of Corsica: How 2023 Drivers Study Historic Tarmac Rallies
articles.summary: A reflective feature on the legacy of Tour de Corse and how modern drivers still learn from classic footage.
articles.content: Even though the Tour de Corse is absent from the current calendar, its narrow, twisting roads still shape how drivers prepare for modern tarmac events like Rally Croatia and Rally Spain...

articles.title: The Data Revolution in Rallying: How Teams Analysed 2023
articles.summary: How WRC engineers used data, simulation, and onboard libraries to unlock performance in 2023.
articles.content: Modern rally teams are data companies as much as they are racing outfits. In 2023, several squads expanded their analysis departments, using simulation tools that would have been unthinkable a decade ago...

articles.title: Why Long Onboards Still Matter in 2023
articles.summary: A feature on how 10+ minute onboard videos remain invaluable for fans and drivers in the 2023 season.
articles.content: In an era of short clips and highlights, long-form onboard videos still offer unmatched insight into driver technique and stage evolution. The 2019 and 2023 seasons in particular produced a treasure trove of extended runs...

articles.title: After Group B: How Rallying Rewrote Its Safety Rulebook
articles.summary: Historical analysis of how the end of Group B forced fundamental changes in rally car and spectator safety.
articles.content: The cancellation of Group B at the end of 1986 marked a turning point. Power outputs were reined in, stage security tightened, and car construction standards rewritten. Over the next decade, the sport shifted its priorities...

articles.title: Sébastien Ogier on Legacy, Part-Time Campaigns, and Monte Carlo Magic
articles.summary: An extended sit-down with Sébastien Ogier discussing his legacy and special relationship with the Monte Carlo Rally.
articles.content: With multiple world titles and a record number of Monte Carlo wins, Sébastien Ogier is in a reflective mood. We sat down with him to discuss how his approach has changed, why Monte Carlo still motivates him, and what’s next...

articles.title: Opinion: What the WRC Must Do After 2025
articles.summary: A fan-focused opinion piece on calendar balance, sustainability, and car regulations beyond 2025.
articles.content: As the current technical cycle approaches its end, the WRC faces big decisions. How many gravel vs tarmac events should the calendar include, how green must the fuel be, and how can privateer entries be encouraged again?

articles.title: Rally1 2025 Updates: What’s New Under the Skin
articles.summary: News breakdown of the 2025 Rally1 update package and what it means for the season.
articles.content: The 2025 Rally1 update kit brings revised aero, updated hybrid maps, and stricter cost caps. We break down what has changed and which teams look best prepared ahead of Monte Carlo...

articles.title: 2019 vs 2023: Comparing Two Modern Classic WRC Seasons
articles.summary: An analytical comparison of the 2019 and 2023 seasons, focusing on competitiveness, reliability, and onboard gold.
articles.content: Both 2019 and 2023 produced gripping title fights, iconic onboard moments, and debate over balance of performance. But how do they stack up when you dig into stage times, retirements, and powerstage points?

cars.name: Toyota GR Yaris Rally1
cars.description: Toyota’s first Rally1-spec hybrid WRC car, debuting in 2022 with a strong mix of reliability and performance on all surfaces.

cars.name: Hyundai i20 N Rally1
cars.description: Hyundai’s Rally1 challenger featuring aggressive aero and a powerful hybrid boost system for the 2022 era and beyond.

cars.name: Ford Puma Rally1
cars.description: M-Sport Ford’s compact crossover-based Rally1 car, known for its strong tarmac performance and distinctive sound.

cars.name: Subaru Impreza WRC 2004
cars.description: The 2004 iteration of Subaru’s iconic blue and gold World Rally Car, driven by stars like Petter Solberg with around 315 hp.

cars.name: Mitsubishi Lancer WRC 2003
cars.description: Mitsubishi’s early-2000s World Rally Car, featuring active differentials and classic WRC styling with just over 300 hp.

cars.name: Škoda Fabia R5 Evo
cars.description: One of the most successful customer rally cars ever built, dominating WRC2 and national championships worldwide.

cars.name: Citroën DS3 WRC
cars.description: Citroën’s first 1.6-litre turbo World Rally Car, taking multiple titles with Sébastien Loeb and Sébastien Ogier.

cars.name: Lancia Delta S4
cars.description: A legendary Group B monster with both a supercharger and turbocharger, famous for its explosive performance and short lifespan.

cars.name: Toyota Celica GT-Four ST185
cars.description: A Group A classic that brought multiple world titles to Toyota, particularly strong on gravel and snow.

cars.name: Ford Escort RS1800 Mk2
cars.description: An iconic rear-wheel-drive rally car beloved in historic and national championships for its balance and sound.

drivers.bio: Multiple-time World Rally Champion known for his precision, adaptability, and a record number of Monte Carlo Rally wins.
The 2019 World Rally Champion, famed for his relentless speed and commitment on fast gravel rallies.
Long-time Hyundai spearhead with multiple vice-championships and victories on both tarmac and gravel.
Welsh WRC frontrunner with a smooth driving style and several near-miss title campaigns.
Record-breaking young World Rally Champion, known for his car control and calm under pressure.
Experienced tarmac specialist and dependable points scorer, often drafted for partial WRC programmes.
Finnish driver with victories at the top level and a spectacular, attacking style on fast gravel.
Norwegian rally winner and fan favourite, known for his commitment and spectacular driving style.
One of the most experienced WRC drivers ever, later becoming Toyota’s team principal while still contesting selected events.
1981 World Rally Champion and rally legend, famous for his attacking driving style and later political career.
Two-time World Rally Champion and tarmac master, key figure in Audi’s Group B campaigns.
Trailblazing French driver who became the first woman to win a World Rally Championship event.

events.name: 2025 Rallye Monte-Carlo
events.description: Traditional WRC season opener with unpredictable winter conditions in the French Alps and around Monaco.

events.name: 2025 Rally Sweden
events.description: High-speed winter rally featuring snowbanks and studded tyres on frozen roads.

events.name: 2025 Rally México
events.description: Hot and high-altitude gravel stages that test engine cooling and reliability.

events.name: 2025 Rally Croatia
events.description: Twisty, bumpy tarmac stages around Zagreb with frequent surface changes and corner cuts.

events.name: 2025 Vodafone Rally de Portugal
events.description: Iconic gravel rally featuring Fafe’s famous jump and rough, rutted roads on the second pass.

events.name: 2025 Rally Italia Sardegna
events.description: Rough, narrow gravel stages on the island of Sardinia, punishing cars and rewarding precise driving.

events.name: 2025 Safari Rally Kenya
events.description: A modern take on the classic Safari Rally with rough gravel, wildlife, and spectacular scenery around Lake Naivasha.

events.name: 2025 Rally Estonia
events.description: Fast, flowing gravel stages with big jumps and crests, often compared to a modern Rally Finland.

events.name: 2025 Secto Rally Finland
events.description: The legendary fast gravel rally with countless jumps and committed high-speed corners through Finnish forests.

events.name: 2024 Rally di Roma Capitale
events.description: European Rally Championship tarmac round based around Rome with technical mountain stages.

events.name: 2024 Lake Finland National Rally
events.description: Popular Finnish national gravel rally using classic forest stages near lakes and rolling hills.

events.name: 2026 Rallye Monte-Carlo
events.description: The 2026 edition of the classic Monte Carlo Rally, opening the season with hybrid Rally1 cars tackling icy mountain passes.

stages.name: Ouninpohja 2025
stages.elevationProfile: Fast roller-coaster of crests and jumps with flowing elevation changes through dense forest.
stages.description: The legendary Ouninpohja stage in its 2025 configuration, combining blind crests, big jumps, and high-speed corners.

stages.name: Ruuhimäki Power Stage
stages.elevationProfile: Mixture of technical forest sections and a spectacular jump-filled arena-style finish.
stages.description: A modern Rally Finland power stage with a mix of fast flowing sections and man-made jumps near the finish.

stages.name: Päihiala Lake Loop
stages.elevationProfile: Rolling lakeside roads with medium-speed sweeps and occasional tight junctions over crests.
stages.description: A classic Finnish national rally stage running along forest tracks between lakes, rewarding precise car placement.

stages.name: Moksi 2025
stages.elevationProfile: Combination of narrow technical forest roads and faster open sections with gentle crests.
stages.description: A well-known Jyvaskylä-area test piece, mixing tighter forest corners with quicker flowing sections.

stages.name: Lahti Lake Sprint
stages.elevationProfile: Short, punchy stage hugging the shoreline with several narrow bridges and cambered corners.
stages.description: A compact Finnish gravel sprint used in the national Lake Finland Rally, ideal for spectators near the city.

stages.name: Oyani Plains
stages.elevationProfile: Open savannah roads with ruts, rocks, and water crossings, plus sweeping elevation changes.
stages.description: A punishing Safari Rally Kenya stage across open plains where dust, wildlife, and weather can all play a role.

stages.name: Lake Elmenteita
stages.elevationProfile: Rough gravel tracks skirting the lake shore with soft sand sections and rocky climbs.
stages.description: A scenic but demanding stage featuring volcanic rock sections and softer sand that can trap unwary crews.

stages.name: Fafe 2025 Power Stage
stages.elevationProfile: Steady climb to the famous jump, with a mix of medium and high-speed corners along ridgelines.
stages.description: The iconic Fafe stage, finishing over the famous jump packed with fans waving Portuguese flags.

stages.name: Monte Lerno - Micky’s Jump
stages.elevationProfile: Rough, narrow gravel with rocky outcrops, steep climbs, and the famous Micky’s Jump crest.
stages.description: One of Sardinia’s toughest tests, combining car-breaking roughness with a spectacular jump section.

stages.name: Kanepi Blast
stages.elevationProfile: Fast, flowing Estonian gravel with sweeping crests and long committed corners.
stages.description: A modern high-speed stage typical of Rally Estonia, demanding bravery and accurate pacenotes.

stages.name: El Chocolate 2025
stages.elevationProfile: Long climb to high altitude with tight hairpins, cliff edges, and twisty switchbacks.
stages.description: A signature Rally México stage combining altitude, heat, and narrow mountain roads lined with drop-offs.

stages.name: Ulrika Snow Sprint
stages.elevationProfile: Flat but ultra-fast forest roads with snowbanks, requiring precise use of studded tyres.
stages.description: A quick blast in the forests near Umeå, where leaning on the snowbanks can be the fastest line.

stages.name: Platak Pass
stages.elevationProfile: Climbing mountain road with mixed grip, patches of gravel drag-out, and tightening hairpins.
stages.description: A technical tarmac stage where changing grip and cut corners make tyre choice critical.

stages.name: Col de Turini Night Run
stages.elevationProfile: Hairpin-filled climb and descent over a high mountain pass with potential ice and snow patches.
stages.description: The classic Monte Carlo night stage over Col de Turini, combining tarmac hairpins with unpredictable winter conditions.

stages.name: Col de Turini Daylight
stages.elevationProfile: Mixed dry and icy patches on a narrow mountain road with steep drop-offs and no room for error.
stages.description: A 2026 configuration of the Turini stage run partly in daylight, still treacherous with changing grip.

stages.name: Fumone Hillclimb
stages.elevationProfile: Steep hillside road with tight hairpins, stone walls, and narrow village sections.
stages.description: A signature Rally di Roma Capitale stage climbing towards the hilltop town of Fumone.

stages.name: Järvi Test Loop
stages.elevationProfile: Short loop mixing compacted gravel and short tarmac links between farmhouses.
stages.description: A mixed-surface shakedown-style loop used for testing setups before the main Finnish national stages.

videos.title: 2019 Rally Finland SS14 Ouninpohja – Kalle Rovanperä Flat Out Onboard
videos.description: Full high-speed onboard from Ouninpohja, showing Kalle Rovanperä attacking the legendary Finnish jumps in his R5 car.

videos.title: 2019 Rallye Monte-Carlo – Col de Turini Night Stage Onboard with Sébastien Ogier
videos.description: Long-form onboard of Sébastien Ogier tackling an icy Col de Turini at night, with constant surface changes and spectators lining the hairpins.

videos.title: 2019 Rally Chile – Ott Tänak Onboard Through the Forests
videos.description: Full stage onboard from Ott Tänak on Rally Chile’s fast and flowing forest roads, with changing grip and heavy compression zones.

videos.title: 2019 Rally Sweden – Snowbank Attack Onboard with Thierry Neuville
videos.description: Extended onboard on a snowy Swedish stage, with Neuville leaning on snowbanks and carrying huge speed through the forests.

videos.title: 2019 Rally México – El Chocolate Long Stage Onboard with Sébastien Ogier
videos.description: Hot and dusty onboard from El Chocolate, with hairpins, cliffs and high-altitude engine strain fully on display.

videos.title: 2019 Rally Spain – Shakedown Onboard Highlights
videos.description: Shorter shakedown onboard clip from Rally Spain, focusing on a few key corners and pacenote calls.

videos.title: 2025 Rallye Monte-Carlo – Ott Tänak Night Run over Col de Turini
videos.description: Rally1 hybrid onboard as Ott Tänak tackles the classic Turini night stage in the 2025 season opener.

videos.title: 2025 Rally Sweden – Ulrika Snow Sprint Onboard with Kalle Rovanperä
videos.description: Flat-out blast through the Ulrika stage, showing just how much speed the studded tyres can carry on ice.

videos.title: 2025 Rally México – Full El Chocolate Onboard with Thierry Neuville
videos.description: Complete run of the long El Chocolate stage in a Rally1 car, including onboard telemetry and pacenote audio.

videos.title: 2025 Rally de Portugal – Fafe Power Stage Onboard with Elfyn Evans
videos.description: End-of-rally power stage onboard including the iconic Fafe jump and huge crowds.

videos.title: 2025 Rally Italia Sardegna – Monte Lerno / Micky’s Jump with Dani Sordo
videos.description: Rough, technical onboard over Monte Lerno, featuring the famous Micky’s Jump and tight rocky sections.

videos.title: 2025 Safari Rally Kenya – Oyani Plains Onboard with Sébastien Ogier
videos.description: Long Safari onboard across rutted plains, water crossings and fesh-fesh dust, highlighting just how tough modern Safari stages are.

videos.title: 2025 Secto Rally Finland – Ruuhimäki Power Stage Onboard with Kalle Rovanperä
videos.description: Onboard from the jump-filled Ruuhimäki stage, including the spectacular arena-style finish section.

videos.title: 2024 Rally di Roma Capitale – Fumone Hillclimb Onboard with Esapekka Lappi
videos.description: Tight and twisty tarmac onboard from the Fumone stage, threading a Rally2 car between stone walls and village houses.

videos.title: 2024 Lake Finland Rally – Päihiala Lake Loop Onboard with Mads Østberg
videos.description: National-level Finnish gravel onboard, with Mads Østberg pushing hard between the lakes on classic flowing roads.

videos.title: 1986 Rally de Portugal – Fafe Jump Onboard in Lancia Delta S4
videos.description: Historic Group B onboard over the famous Fafe jump, showcasing the brutal acceleration and sound of the Delta S4.

videos.title: 1992 1000 Lakes Rally – Classic Finnish Gravel Onboard in Celica GT-Four
videos.description: Onboard from an early-1990s Toyota Celica GT-Four blasting through fast Finnish gravel, with period pacenotes and camera angles.

videos.title: 2026 Rallye Monte-Carlo – Col de Turini Daylight Run with Kalle Rovanperä
videos.description: Onboard from the 2026 daylight configuration of Turini, mixing dry tarmac and icy patches in a Rally1 hybrid.
